overview: This deck leverages creature sacrifice outlets to fuel The Gitrog Monster’s card draw and discard, creating a self-sustaining engine of card advantage and graveyard recursion. It wins by out-valuing opponents through relentless resource generation, milling them out, or overwhelming them with resilient threats like Golgari Grave-Troll.
win_conditions: Golgari Grave-Troll (provides a 4/4 body and recursion on death), milling opponents to zero, or attacking with a board of value.
Use recursion to keep Grave-Troll or other threats in play while continuing to sacrifice for card advantage.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritize basics or fetchable duals), at least one sacrifice outlet (Icetill Explorer, Golgari Grave-Troll, or ritual/discard spells), and early interaction.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have zero lands, only non-basic lands without fetch support, or a hand of 3+ high-cost spells with no early plays.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cheap discard or sacrifice outlet if available.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast The Gitrog Monster or a sacrificial creature.
Sequence mana to ensure black availability for discard/sacrifice and green for mana acceleration or threats.
Prioritize establishing the sacrifice outlet before committing to the Monster.
mid_game: Treat your graveyard as a secondary hand
cycle creatures through sacrifice outlets to fuel Gitrog’s draw/discard loop.
Balance mana between casting spells, playing lands, and holding creatures for sacrifice.
Avoid over-investing in recursion if the board state allows you to simply sacrifice for immediate value.
Commit threats when you have card advantage to answer responses or when opponents are low on interaction.
Hold back if you’re resource-starved or facing a sweep.
Use discard to disrupt key combo pieces or counterspells before they resolve.
late_game: Golgari Grave-Troll (provides a 4/4 body and recursion on death), milling opponents to zero, or attacking with a board of value.
Use recursion to keep Grave-Troll or other threats in play while continuing to sacrifice for card advantage.
Go all-in when you have a card advantage lead, can answer responses, or opponents are at low life/mill threshold.
Commit when you’ve established a board that can absorb one or two removal spells.

sequencing_tips: Common mistakes include sacrificing too early without a plan, ignoring graveyard hate, and over-committing to The Gitrog Monster without protection.
Unique interaction: Golgari Grave-Troll’s sacrifice can trigger The Gitrog Monster’s draw/discard, then you can use its death trigger to return itself or another creature, creating a recursive loop.
Fetch lands can tutor for basics to enable Gitrog’s exile ability.
Always track opponents’ graveyard sizes to know when milling becomes a win condition.

matchups.advice: Use discard to remove counterspells from hand, cast spells in response to counters (if possible), or rely on uncounterable threats and recursion. Don’t over-commit to one spell; spread risk across multiple threats.

Apply early pressure with discard and removal. Don’t let them get too far ahead in mana; use board wipes and efficient removal to reset the board. Prioritize targeting their mana sources and key ramp spells.
Stabilize early with discard, board wipes, and efficient removal. Don’t over-commit to the engine until you’ve stabilized the board. Use sacrifice outlets to fuel Gitrog while answering threats.
Apply pressure with sacrifice outlets and discard to disrupt their hand. Out-value them with card draw and recursion. Don’t over-commit to one threat; force them to use multiple answers.
Win the resource war by prioritizing graveyard protection, discard, and targeted removal. Out-value them in card advantage and recursion. Use discard to remove their key threats and recursion spells.

common_threats: Monitor opponents for graveyard hate, board wipes, and combo assembly.
Note when players are protecting their graveyard or hoarding removal.
Watch for mana bases that indicate ramp or control, and adjust your discard/removal targets accordingly.
